stem: An engineer takes 15 measurements of fuel level using a fuel gauge. The measurements have a mean of 20.9 % and a standard deviation of 2.4 %. However, calibration tests reveal that a fuel gauge systematically underestimates the true value by between 3.3% and 9.0%. Estimate the true fuel level and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 20.9 %. After correcting for the 6.2% bias: ≈ 22.3 %. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (3.3%-9.0%) and sampling variability: [20.4, 24.2]. Point estimate: 22.3 %.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
